sourceMovie.title: All Is Lost
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto All Is Lost (2013) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: In the Heart of the Sea
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If All Is Lost worked for you, In the Heart of the Sea (2015) is a strong next pick. The two films share action and adventure throughlines and momentum and clean stakes, even though the stories go in different directions.

faq.question: What should I watch after All Is Lost?
faq.answer: Try In the Heart of the Sea, The Perfect Storm, Pirates of the Caribbean: At World's End—each shares DNA with All Is Lost while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is All Is Lost?
faq.answer: All Is Lost (2013) is best described as Action, Adventure, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is All Is Lost worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
